source.title: Clinical Utility of Oncotype DX Testing in Synchronous Bilateral and Unilateral Multifocal Breast Cancer.

finding.description: RS risk-category discordance occurred in 21% of bilateral and 24.7% of unilateral multifocal breast cancer cases.
finding.effect_summary: RS risk-category discordance was found in 21% of bilateral and 24.7% of unilateral cases.

source_quote: Among 131 patients (38 bilateral, 93 unilateral multifocal), RS risk-category discordance occurred in 21 and 24.7%, respectively, leading to chemotherapy escalation in half of the discordant cases.

source.title: Clinical Utility of Oncotype DX Testing in Synchronous Bilateral and Unilateral Multifocal Breast Cancer.

finding.description: In bilateral disease, progesterone receptor score difference was significantly associated with absolute RS difference (β = 1.95; 95% CI, 0.44-3.46; P = 0.013).
finding.effect_summary: Progesterone receptor score difference was significantly associated with RS difference in bilateral disease.

source_quote: In bilateral disease, only the progesterone receptor score difference was significantly associated with the absolute RS difference (β = 1.95; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.44-3.46; P = 0.013).

source.title: Clinical Utility of Oncotype DX Testing in Synchronous Bilateral and Unilateral Multifocal Breast Cancer.

finding.description: In unilateral disease, absolute RS difference was independently associated with differences in HER2, progesterone receptor, and estrogen receptor scores (all P < 0.05).
finding.effect_summary: RS difference in unilateral disease was associated with HER2, progesterone, and estrogen receptor score differences.

source_quote: In unilateral disease, absolute RS difference was independently associated with differences in HER2, progesterone receptor, and estrogen receptor scores (all P < 0.05).

source.title: Clinical Utility of Oncotype DX Testing in Synchronous Bilateral and Unilateral Multifocal Breast Cancer.

finding.description: Recurrence score correlation between paired tumors was higher in unilateral multifocal than in bilateral disease (r = 0.66 vs. 0.36).
finding.effect_summary: Higher RS correlation in unilateral multifocal than bilateral disease.

source_quote: Recurrence score correlation between paired tumors was higher in unilateral multifocal than in bilateral disease (r = 0.66 vs. 0.36).
